task_completion.description: Verify that Einstein's 1905 paper is in the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero 'Trash' view, the paper titled 'On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies' should be visible in the item list.

task_completion.description: Verify that Watson & Crick's 1953 paper is in the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero 'Trash' view, the paper titled 'Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids: A Structure for Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid' should be visible in the item list.

task_completion.description: Verify that Church's 1936 paper is in the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero 'Trash' view, the paper titled 'An Unsolvable Problem of Elementary Number Theory' should be visible in the item list.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Shannon & Weaver 1949 book is in the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero 'Trash' view, the item titled 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication' (note the 'The' at the beginning) should be visible in the item list.

task_completion.description: Verify that Shannon's 1948 paper was kept in the library and NOT moved to the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The paper titled 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' (note the 'A' at the beginning) must NOT appear in the Zotero 'Trash' view. It should be visible if the agent shows the 'My Library' view.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Trash contains exactly 4 items and has not been emptied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Zotero 'Trash' view should show exactly 4 items in the list. If the Trash was emptied, it would be empty. Alternatively, the 'My Library' view should show exactly 14 items remaining.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Zotero graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Zotero application window being clicked and navigated, rather than terminal commands modifying the Zotero SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual system 'Trash' within Zotero.
integrity.visual_evidence: The items should be located in the built-in 'Trash' bin at the bottom of the left pane in Zotero, not in a manually created collection or folder named 'Trash'.

integrity.description: The agent actually moved the items, rather than just searching for them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the items inside the Trash view or being dragged/deleted, not just highlighted in the main library search results.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Zotero library starts with 18 papers. Exactly 4 papers must be moved to the Trash: 'On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies' (Einstein, 1905), 'Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids: A Structure for Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid' (Watson & Crick, 1953), 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication' (Shannon & Weaver, 1949 book), and 'An Unsolvable Problem of Elementary Number Theory' (Church, 1936). The paper 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' (Shannon, 1948) is a critical keep and must NOT be in the Trash. The final state should show exactly 4 items in the Trash and 14 items in the main library. The Trash must not be emptied.
